title: koeleria cristata subsp. krylovii
intro: Koeleria cristata subsp. krylovii, also known as Krylov's Koeleria, is a subspecies of perennial grass in the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer properties. Koeleria cristata subsp. krylovii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
